dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7544 IH: Illegal Alien Animal Abuser Removal Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Mace introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend the Immigration and Nationality Act to provide that an alien convicted of an offense involving animal cruelty is inadmissible and deportable, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Illegal Alien Animal Abuser Removal Act of 2026.

section.header: Inadmissibility and deportability related to animal cruelty

subsection.text: Section 212(a)(2) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1182(a)(2)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: Any alien who has been convicted of, who admits having committed, or who admits committing acts constituting the essential elements of—

clause.text: an offense under sections 48 or 49 of title 18, United States Code; or

an offense under State, Tribal, or local law, an essential element of which is animal cruelty, animal abuse, or animal fighting, regardless of whether such offense is classified as a misdemeanor or felony under State, Tribal, or local law,

subsection.text: Section 237(a)(2) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1227(a)(2)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: Any alien who has been convicted of—

clause.text: an offense under sections 48 or 49 of title 18, United States Code; or

an offense under State, Tribal, or local law, an essential element of which is animal cruelty, animal abuse, or animal fighting, regardless of whether such offense is classified as a misdemeanor or felony under State, Tribal, or local law,
